tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

For consumers of the SDK we recommend visiting our public developer docs at https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/ or our versioned developer docs at https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk-for-net. ","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","language":"C#","stargazers_count":5962,"forks_count":5136,"open_issues_count":1254,"updated_at":"2026-04-09T18:39:58Z","created_at":"2011-12-06T23:32:47Z","topics":["azure","azure-sdk","c-sharp","dotnet","hacktoberfest","microsoft"],"private":false,"fork":false,"archived":false,"default_branch":"main"}]}

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Prepare release Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth 1.0.0-beta.1 (#58012)

Co-authored-by: Matthew Boentoro <mboentoro@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Matthew Boentoro","email":"mattboentoro@yahoo.com","date":"2026-04-09T18:39:40Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-09T18:39:40Z"}},"author":{"login":"mattboentoro","id":34062198,"profile_url":"https://github.com/mattboentoro","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/34062198?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Enrich spans with session ID and conversation ID from OTel baggage (#58003)

* Enrich spans with session ID and conversation ID from OTel baggage

FoundryEnrichmentProcessor.OnStart now reads two baggage keys and stamps
them as independent span attributes on all spans (root + framework child):

- azure.ai.agentserver.session_id  → microsoft.session.id
- azure.ai.agentserver.conversation_id → gen_ai.conversation.id

The two are independent — no fallback between them. Session ID never goes
into gen_ai.conversation.id. Both are set in OnStart because they are
correlation IDs that frameworks won't overwrite (unlike agent identity).

Also updates InvocationsActivitySource to map its session_id parameter to
microsoft.session.id instead of gen_ai.conversation.id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Treat empty/whitespace baggage values as absent

Use string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace instead of null check when reading
session_id and conversation_id from OTel baggage. Prevents stamping
empty span attributes when callers set baggage to "" for absent values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Neehar Duvvuri <neeharduvvuri@Neehars-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Neehar Duvvuri","email":"40341266+needuv@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-09T17:16:40Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-09T17:16:40Z"}},"author":{"login":"needuv","id":40341266,"profile_url":"https://github.com/needuv","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40341266?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"TypeForward Azure.Identity types to Azure.Core to enable per-library AddFooClient extensions (#57200)

* TypeForward Azure.Identity types to Azure.Core to enable per-library AddFooClient extensions

Move all Azure.Identity source code into Azure.Core/src/Identity/ and convert
Azure.Identity into a TypeForwardedTo facade. This enables SDK libraries to
call WithAzureCredential() through their existing Azure.Core dependency
without adding a direct dependency on Azure.Identity.

Resolves #57184

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Sort package references alphabetically in Azure.Core.csproj

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove contradictory #nullable disable from ConfigurableCredentialCache.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Scope analyzer suppressions: replace project-level NoWarn with inline solutions

- AZC0011: scoped #pragma around Broker InternalsVisibleTo lines
- AZC0034: inline #pragma with reason on each moved type declaration
- CA1416: [UnsupportedOSPlatform("browser")] on credential types with
  polyfill for netstandard2.0/net462/net472

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reorder package references: System.* first, then Microsoft.*

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: swap Azure.Identity to ProjectReference in confidentialledger test projects

TODO: Revert to PackageReference after Azure.Core 1.52 ships with Identity types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggested edit: backtick-format Microsoft.Extensions namespaces

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix indentation of UnsupportedOSPlatform attribute in PemReader.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Identity snippet definitions for CI snippet verification

Duplicates 3 snippet regions from Azure.Identity tests into Azure.Core
tests so the snippet generator can find them when processing sdk/core/.
Minor indent diff in DefaultAzureCredential.cs XML docs due to known
snippet generator //@@  bug (azure-sdk-tools#14598).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove contradictory #nullable disable from ImdsRetryDelayStrategy.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CHANGELOG entry for Azure.Identity 1.52.0-beta.1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Azure.Core CHANGELOG entry for Identity type inclusion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: swap Azure.Identity to ProjectReference in Broker src

TODO: Revert to PackageReference after Azure.Core 1.52 ships with Identity types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix snippet formatting: use fake types instead of //@@  for proper indentation

Replace //@@  lines with compiled code using nested fake BlobClient and
EventHubProducerClient classes. This avoids the snippet generator's
//@@  indentation bug and produces correctly formatted XML docs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix export-api: add --follow-type-forwards to resolve forwarded types

GenAPI's --follow-type-forwards flag resolves TypeForwardedTo attributes
and includes the forwarded type members in the API listing. This restores
the full API surface in Azure.Identity's listing files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Azure.Core API listings to include Identity types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clarify CHANGELOG: explain version alignment with Azure.Core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add TypeForwardedFrom attributes and update API listings

Add [TypeForwardedFrom("Azure.Identity, ...")] to all 59 public types
that moved from Azure.Identity to Azure.Core. This ensures serialization
compatibility for any types that were serialized with the old assembly name.

Regenerated API listings for both Azure.Core and Azure.Identity to include
the new TypeForwardedFrom attributes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add body sanitizer for stale EventHub recordings in HealthcareApis tests

Same fix as #57317 — needed here to unblock CI integration batch.

Ref #57316

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Ignore HealthcareApis tests incompatible with UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients

Same fix as #57317. Ref #57316

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Suppress CA1416 for nameof() references to browser-unsupported types

The merge from main changed nameof() targets from local properties to
AzurePipelinesCredential members, which triggers CA1416 because that
class is annotated [UnsupportedOSPlatform("browser")]. Since nameof()
is compile-time only and does not invoke any platform-specific API,
a scoped #pragma disable is the correct fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add migration guide for Identity type consolidation and bump version to 1.53.0

Add MigrationGuide.md covering CS0433 scenarios when Azure.Core 1.53+
ships with Identity types and projects reference older Azure.Identity.
Bump Azure.Identity version from 1.52.0-beta.1 to 1.53.0-beta.1 to
align with Azure.Core.

Resolves #57360

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix broken CS0433 link in MigrationGuide.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update migration guide version ranges per review feedback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Code-fence Azure.Core and Azure.Identity throughout migration guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Regenerate API listings after merge from main

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix formatting in Identity source files

Run dotnet format to fix indentation alignment across all Identity
source files moved to Azure.Core.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove dead BrokeredAuthentication InternalsVisibleTo entries

Azure.Identity.BrokeredAuthentication was the predecessor to
Azure.Identity.Broker before it was GA'd. The project no longer
exists so these IVT entries are unnecessary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Strip CCI dummy type references from API listings

GenAPI's --follow-type-forwards emits [Microsoft.Cci.DummyTypeReference]
on async methods for netstandard2.0 when resolving forwarded types.
Add post-processing to Export-API.ps1 to strip these artifacts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move Identity consolidation entry to Other Changes in changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Code-fence package names in changelog entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add migration guide link to Azure.Identity changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix migration guide link to use SHA ref

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CCI stripping regex to work cross-platform in Export-API.ps1

Use line-based filtering instead of multiline regex to strip
[Microsoft.Cci.DummyTypeReference] artifacts, which is more robust
across PowerShell on different platforms.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add diagnostic logging to CCI stripping in Export-API.ps1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Strip CCI artifacts from all API listings including transitive deps

GenAPI regenerates Azure.Identity listings as a transitive dependency
when exporting other service directories. The CCI stripping was only
scoped to the current service directory's files, so later Export-API
runs for other services would overwrite the stripped file with CCI
artifacts. Now also scan all sdk/*/*/api/*.cs files for CCI artifacts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Scope CCI stripping to Azure.Identity API listings only

Instead of scanning all api listings, target only Azure.Identity which
is the only project using --follow-type-forwards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"m-nash","email":"64171366+m-nash@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-09T17:02:04Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-09T17:02:04Z"}},"author":{"login":"m-nash","id":64171366,"profile_url":"https://github.com/m-nash","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Sync eng/common directory with azure-sdk-tools for PR 15041 (#58002)

resource.text:
# Microsoft Azure PostgreSQL Auth client library for .NET

The `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` library provides Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) authentication support for the [Npgsql](https://www.npgsql.org/) PostgreSQL driver. It enables passwordless authentication to Azure Database for PostgreSQL using Azure Identity credentials.

[Source code](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/src) | [Package (NuGet)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth) | [Product documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/postgresql/)

## Getting started

### Install the package

Install the client library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):

```dotnetcli
dotnet add package Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth --prerelease
```

### Prerequisites

- An [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/)
- An [Azure Database for PostgreSQL](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/postgresql/) server with Entra ID authentication enabled
- An Entra ID administrator configured on the PostgreSQL server
- The application's Entra ID identity created as a database user with appropriate permissions

### Authenticate the client

This library extends the Npgsql `NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder` with Entra ID authentication. Use any `TokenCredential` from [Azure.Identity](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/overview/azure/identity-readme):

```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate
var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();
var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require");
builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential);
```

## Key concepts

### Entra ID authentication

The library configures token-based authentication by:
1. Extracting the username from the Entra ID token claims
2. Setting up a password provider that supplies fresh tokens for each connection

### Supported identity types

- **User identities** — extracted from `upn`, `preferred_username`, or `unique_name` claims
- **Managed identities** — extracted from the `xms_mirid` claim
- **Service principals** — extracted from available token claims

### Thread safety

The `UseEntraAuthentication` and `UseEntraAuthenticationAsync` extension methods configure the `NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder` and are intended to be called once during setup. The resulting `NpgsqlDataSource` is thread-safe per Npgsql documentation.

### Additional concepts
<!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR -->
[Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |
[Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |
[Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) |
[Mocking](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/unit-testing-mocking) |
[Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/)
<!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR -->

## Examples

### Synchronous authentication

```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync
var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();
var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require");
builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential);
```

### Asynchronous authentication

```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Async
var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential();
var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require");
await builder.UseEntraAuthenticationAsync(credential);
```

## Troubleshooting

### Common errors

- **"Could not determine username from token claims"** — The token does not contain a recognized username claim. Ensure the identity has the correct permissions and the token contains one of: `upn`, `xms_mirid`, `preferred_username`, or `unique_name`.
- **`NotSupportedException` when calling `Build()`** — A password is already set in the connection string. Remove the `Password` parameter when using Entra ID authentication.

### Logging

This library uses the standard Azure SDK logging mechanisms. For details on configuring logging, see [Logging with the Azure SDK for .NET](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/logging).

## Next steps

- [Azure Database for PostgreSQL documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/postgresql/)
- [Azure Identity documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/overview/azure/identity-readme)
- [Npgsql documentation](https://www.npgsql.org/doc/)

## Contributing

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit <https://cla.microsoft.com>.

When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the bot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/). For more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/) or contact [opencode@microsoft.com](mailto:opencode@microsoft.com) with any additional questions or comments.

# Release History

## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2026-04-09)

### Features Added

- Entra ID authentication extension for Npgsql PostgreSQL driver
- Synchronous and asynchronous `UseEntraAuthentication` extension methods for `NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder`
- Automatic username extraction from JWT token claims (`upn`, `xms_mirid`, `preferred_username`, `unique_name`)
- Password provider integration with Npgsql data source builder for token-based authentication

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` **Service directory:** `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58012) by @mattboentoro ## What Changed The `Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth` 1.0.0-beta.1 release preparation commit was pushed, marking the first public beta of this library. It provides Entra ID (AAD) passwordless authentication for the Npgsql PostgreSQL driver via `UseEntraAuthentication` / `UseEntraAuthenticationAsync` extension methods on `NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder`. ## Gaps Found - **Incomplete examples in README**: Both the "Synchronous authentication" and "Asynchronous authentication" sections end at `builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential)` without calling `builder.Build()` — users are left without the step needed to actually create a usable `NpgsqlDataSource` - **No end-to-end query example in README**: `samples/Sample01_HelloWorld.md` shows the full flow (`Build()` → `OpenConnectionAsync()` → `ExecuteScalarAsync()`), but the main README `Examples` section never demonstrates how to execute a query against the database - **Explicit username pattern missing from README**: The `Key concepts` section explains username extraction but provides no code example for the explicit-username override pattern (passing `Username=my-db-role` in the connection string), which is a common advanced use case - **Redundant "Authenticate the client" and "Synchronous authentication" snippets**: `Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate` and `Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync` (backed by `ReadMeSnippets.Authenticate()` and `ReadMeSnippets.SynchronousAuthentication()`) render identical code in the README, wasting space and confusing readers
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/README.md`** 1. **Collapse the "Authenticate the client" snippet** — replace the inline code block with prose pointing readers to the Examples section (removing the duplicate `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Authenticate` placeholder), OR merge it with the sync example below. 2. **Extend the "Synchronous authentication" example** to include `builder.Build()`: Replace: ````C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync ``` with a snippet that also calls `builder.Build()` (update the region in the test file accordingly — see Step 2). 3. **Extend the "Asynchronous authentication" example** similarly to include `builder.Build()`. 4. **Add a "Query the database" subsection** under `## Examples` with a new snippet placeholder: ```markdown ### Query the database Once the data source is built, open a connection and execute queries as usual: ```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query ``` ``` 5. **Add an "Explicit database role name" subsection** under `## Examples`: ```markdown ### Explicit database role name If the PostgreSQL role name differs from the Entra ID identity name, include `Username` in the connection string. The library will skip token-based username extraction and use the provided value: ```C# Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **File: `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/tests/Samples/ReadMeSnippets.cs`** 1. Update `SynchronousAuthentication()` to include `Build()` inside the region: ```csharp [Test] public async Task SynchronousAuthentication() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=localhost;Database=testdb"); #endif builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #endregion } ``` 2. Update `AsynchronousAuthentication()` similarly: ```csharp [Test] public async Task AsynchronousAuthentication() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Async #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=localhost;Database=testdb"); #endif await builder.UseEntraAuthenticationAsync(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #endregion } ``` 3. Add new `ReadMeQuery()` method for the query snippet (wrapped in `#if SNIPPET` because it requires a live database): ```csharp [Test] [Category("Live")] public async Task ReadMeQuery() { #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder("Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;SSL Mode=Require"); builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); await using var dataSource = builder.Build(); #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query await using var connection = await dataSource.OpenConnectionAsync(); await using var cmd = new NpgsqlCommand("SELECT version()", connection); var version = await cmd.ExecuteScalarAsync(); #endregion #else await Task.CompletedTask; #endif } ``` 4. Add new `ReadMeExplicitUsername()` method: ```csharp [Test] public void ReadMeExplicitUsername() { #region Snippet:PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername #if SNIPPET var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var connectionString = "Host=<< YOUR SERVER >>.postgres.database.azure.com;Database=<< YOUR DATABASE >>;Username=my-db-role;SSL Mode=Require"; #else var credential = new TestTokenCredential(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.CreateValidJwtToken(PostgreSqlTestEnvironment.EntraUser)); var connectionString = "Host=localhost;Database=testdb;Username=my-db-role"; #endif var builder = new NpgsqlDataSourceBuilder(connectionString); builder.UseEntraAuthentication(credential); #endregion } ``` Snippet name mapping: | Snippet name | Test method | README section | |---|---|---| | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Sync` | `SynchronousAuthentication` | Examples → Synchronous authentication | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Async` | `AsynchronousAuthentication` | Examples → Asynchronous authentication | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_Query` | `ReadMeQuery` | Examples → Query the database | | `PostgreSqlAuth_ReadMe_ExplicitUsername` | `ReadMeExplicitUsername` | Examples → Explicit database role name | ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No sections need to be added, only the Examples section content needs to be extended as described above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 postgresql` — verify the README is updated with the new snippet content by reviewing `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 postgresql` — no public API changes expected; run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/postgresql/Microsoft.Azure.PostgreSQL.Auth/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [PostgreSQL] Docs: README examples are incomplete — missing Build() and query usage
